claims.claim: Cross-chain bridges with weak trust assumptions (back interoperability only where security is sound)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on on technology and teams; under-weight token price/macro
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Newly vocal on web3 music / creator-economy NFTs (led Sound.xyz Series A, 7/12; Tiers launch 9/25) — a consumer/creator sector not in the prediction
Newly emphasized crypto payments × web2 infrastructure (Loop+Stripe 5/10, payments overhaul 9/5) — not anticipated
Backed decentralized information markets (Freatic, 9/20) as a new application area
Endorsed web3 messaging protocols (XMTP/Coinbase Wallet, 7/12) as core infrastructure
Explicitly elevated EigenLayer/restaking (8/24) as a way to extend Ethereum without fragmenting network effects
notes: Nearly the entire technology-first thesis is confirmed and actively acted upon: ZK (Iron Fish mainnet, Lasso/Jolt, Aztec Sandbox), privacy ('hard to imagine mainstream adoption without privacy'), AI×crypto (led Gensyn Series A, AI×crypto podcast), DAOs (PartyDAO), modular/multichain (LayerZero, L2 'permissionless innovation', modular-vs-monolithic Solana debate), and ETH/SOL conviction. DePIN counted confirmed via Gensyn's idle-compute marketplace thesis rather than the original wireless framing. Bridge concern is confirmed indirectly — he champions LayerZero specifically as it adds Google-backed end-to-end security, consistent with backing interop only where the trust model is sound. NEAR, wallet-UX concern, and CeFi concern simply went unaddressed in the window. Risk-on-on-builders stance strongly confirmed by a steady cadence of new lead investments. The main unanticipated moves are a pronounced lean into consumer/creator (music NFTs) and payments infrastructure.
